name: visitor_to_signup_rate
definition: Unique signups divided by unique visitors over a rolling 14-day window, attributed by first-touch session.
target_hint: B2B SaaS PLG: 2–5% cold, 8–15% warm/email. Ecommerce: 1.5–3%. Below 1% with >1k visitors/day = page problem, not traffic problem.

name: hero_engagement_rate
definition: Percent of sessions where the user scrolls past the hero OR clicks the primary/secondary CTA within the hero viewport.
target_hint: >70% healthy; <50% means the headline or visual fails the 5-second test.

name: cta_click_through_rate
definition: Clicks on the primary CTA divided by sessions, deduped per user per day.
target_hint: Cold traffic: 8–15%. Warm traffic: 20–35%. Below 5% is a copy or placement problem.

name: form_completion_rate
definition: Form submits divided by form starts (first field focus), tracked per form.
target_hint: >60% with ≤3 fields. <40% means too many fields or unclear value at submit.

name: qualified_signup_rate
definition: Signups that match ICP (verified by enrichment or post-signup survey) divided by total signups.
target_hint: >60% for narrow ICPs. <40% means the headline is too broad or paid targeting is off.

name: time_to_first_value
definition: Median time from signup to the first activation event (e.g. first project created, first integration connected).
target_hint: <10 minutes for self-serve SaaS. Every additional minute correlates with day-7 retention loss.

name: page_load_lcp
definition: Largest Contentful Paint at the 75th percentile of real-user sessions.
target_hint: <2.5s. Each additional second above this typically costs 5–10% of conversions.

name: demo_to_paid_conversion
definition: Paid customers divided by completed demos (sales-led path only), measured at a 60-day attribution window.
target_hint: B2B SaaS: 20–35% healthy. <15% indicates lead-qual or demo-script problems, not page problems.
